---
title: "Example Current Findings Report"
subtitle: "A short non-peer-reviewed scientific report"
author:
  - name: Steven Roberts
    affiliation: University of Washington
date: "2026-06-17"
date-modified: "2026-06-17"
description: "Example report showing the standard structure for Current Findings."
categories: [example, methods, open-science]
status: "Not peer reviewed"
report_type: "Working manuscript"
lab: "Roberts Lab"
project: "Current Findings"
species: []
topic: [open science, scientific communication]
github_repo: "https://github.com/RobertsLab/current-findings"
data_url: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Status

This report is a Roberts Lab working manuscript. It has not been peer reviewed.

It is shared to make small scientific efforts, preliminary analyses, technical observations, and exploratory work openly available.
:::

## Abstract

This example report demonstrates the standard structure for Current Findings.

## Background

Current Findings provides a public home for small scientific outputs, technical notes, exploratory analyses, field reports, and working manuscripts.

## Methods

Reports are written in Quarto Markdown and rendered as a static website using GitHub Pages.

## Results

Each report appears as a standalone page and is automatically listed on the Reports page.

## Discussion

This format supports open science, transparency, student training, and early sharing of useful scientific work.

## Data and code availability

Source files are available in the RobertsLab `current-findings` GitHub repository.

## Suggested citation

Roberts, S. B. 2026. *Example Current Findings Report*. Current Findings. Available at: https://robertslab.github.io/current-findings/reports/example-report/

## Version history

| Version | Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | 2026-06-17 | Initial draft |
